name: Tandoori Grilled Salmon
tagline: Yogurt-marinated salmon with Indian spice — clinical omega-3 dose per fillet

hero.alt: Orange-pink salmon fillet with tandoori marinade char marks, lemon wedge alongside
hero.caption: tandoori salmon — yogurt-marinated, high-heat grilled

steps.title: Marinate
steps.detail: Pat salmon dry. Whisk yogurt with all marinade ingredients and salt. Coat the salmon and rest 15 minutes at room temperature.
steps.tip: 15 minutes is the sweet spot — too long and the yogurt's acid starts breaking down the fish.

steps.title: Sear hot
steps.detail: Heat a cast-iron pan or grill pan with mustard oil till it just smokes. Place salmon skin-side down. Cook 3 minutes without moving, flip, cook 2 more minutes till just opaque in the middle.
steps.tip: Salmon at 50–52°C internal is perfect — dry salmon is a tragedy.

steps.title: Rest and serve
steps.detail: Move to a plate and let it rest 2 minutes — juices redistribute. Squeeze lemon, sprinkle salt and chaat masala. Serve with sautéed greens and a wedge of lime.
steps.tip: If using oven: 220°C for 10 min on a foil-lined tray works well too.

healthFacts: 150g salmon = 2.5g EPA+DHA — by itself doubles a typical day's omega-3 intake.
Vitamin D content (~570IU per fillet) covers daily needs in a single meal.
Astaxanthin (the pink pigment) is a strong antioxidant that survives cooking.
Selenium (35µg) supports thyroid hormone activation and antioxidant glutathione.
